skill.name: azure-monitor-query-py
skill.description: Guidance and examples for using the Azure Monitor Query SDK for Python with Log Analytics workspaces and Azure Monitor metrics.
skill.summary: Build Python queries for Azure Monitor logs, metrics, batch requests, and common Kusto analysis workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static external command and weak cryptography findings are false positives caused by Markdown code fences, documentation text, and Kusto ordering examples. The only confirmed risk factor is expected environment variable access for Azure workspace and resource identifiers in sample Python code. No prompt injection, malicious intent, data exfiltration, or executable helper scripts were found.

low_findings.title: Expected Environment Variable Access in Examples
low_findings.description: The sample Python code reads Azure workspace and resource identifiers from environment variables. This is expected for Azure Monitor examples, but users should avoid exposing sensitive tenant or subscription details in shared outputs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable reads are directly visible in sample code and are used as Azure identifiers. They do not show secret exfiltration or unauthorized network destinations.

low_findings.title: External Command Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported external command locations are Markdown code fences and documentation examples. The file contains no Ruby backtick execution or executable skill script.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each location corresponds to Markdown fencing or displayed sample content. No interpreted Ruby, shell wrapper, or command construction is present in the skill file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography matches do not identify cryptographic APIs or hash usage. The cited lines contain metadata, query ordering, and Kusto examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines do not contain MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or comparable weak cryptographic operations. The static signal appears to be a substring or formatting match.

content.user_title: Query Azure Monitor Logs and Metrics
content.value_statement: Azure Monitor data often needs correct SDK setup, authentication, and Kusto query structure. This skill provides Python examples for logs, metrics, batch queries, async clients, and common analysis patterns.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to install and import the Azure Monitor Query SDK for Python.
Demonstrates DefaultAzureCredential authentication for Azure Monitor clients.
Provides LogsQueryClient examples for workspace queries, time ranges, batch queries, and partial results.
Provides MetricsQueryClient examples for resource metrics, aggregations, dimensions, definitions, and namespaces.
Includes async client examples for log and metric query workflows.
Lists common Kusto queries for requests, exceptions, slow requests, and top errors.
limitations: Does not provision Azure resources, workspaces, roles, or managed identities.
Does not verify live SDK behavior or Azure API changes during use.
Requires valid Azure credentials and permissions outside the skill.
Examples are documentation snippets, not a packaged application or test suite.

use_cases.title: Investigate Application Errors
use_cases.description: Query Log Analytics tables for exceptions, failed requests, and slow operations during incident response.

use_cases.title: Build Metrics Reports
use_cases.description: Use Python to collect Azure Monitor metrics with selected aggregations, time windows, and dimensions.

use_cases.title: Prototype Observability Scripts
use_cases.description: Create quick Python scripts for workspace queries, batch requests, and DataFrame conversion.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Logs Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Use azure-monitor-query-py to show a simple Python LogsQueryClient example for my workspace. Query recent AppRequests for the last hour and print rows.

prompt_templates.title: Add Time Range Handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Use azure-monitor-query-py to write a Python Log Analytics query with an explicit UTC start and end time. Explain where I should pass the workspace ID.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Resource Metrics
prompt_templates.prompt: Use azure-monitor-query-py to query Azure Monitor metrics for a resource URI. Include average, maximum, minimum, and count aggregations with five-minute granularity.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Async Batch Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use azure-monitor-query-py to design an async Python workflow that runs multiple Log Analytics queries, handles partial results, and closes Azure clients correctly.

output_examples.input: Query recent application requests and summarize status codes.
output_examples.output: A Python-oriented answer that creates a LogsQueryClient, defines a Kusto query for AppRequests, sets a one-hour timespan, and iterates result rows.

output_examples.input: Get CPU and network metrics for an Azure resource.
output_examples.output: A metrics workflow that creates a MetricsQueryClient, reads the resource URI, requests selected metric names, applies granularity, and prints timestamped values.

output_examples.input: Handle failures from a large Log Analytics query.
output_examples.output: Guidance that checks partial and failure statuses, reports the partial error, and recommends smaller time windows or narrower Kusto filters.

best_practices: Use least-privilege Azure roles for the workspace or monitored resource.
Limit time ranges and use Kusto filters before exporting or printing results.
Close async Azure clients and credentials after query operations complete.
anti_patterns: Do not paste tenant IDs, subscription IDs, or query results into public prompts.
Do not run broad queries over long time ranges without filters or aggregation.
Do not assume partial results are complete when Azure Monitor reports warnings.

faq.question: What SDK does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers the Azure Monitor Query SDK for Python, including LogsQueryClient and MetricsQueryClient examples.

faq.question: Can it authenticate to Azure for me?
faq.answer: No. It shows DefaultAzureCredential usage, but credentials and Azure permissions must already be configured.

faq.question: Does it include Kusto query examples?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes common Kusto patterns for requests, exceptions, slow requests, and top errors.

faq.question: Can it return pandas DataFrames?
faq.answer: It shows how to convert a returned table into a pandas DataFrame for local analysis.

faq.question: Does it support async Python examples?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes async client imports, awaited query calls, and client cleanup.

faq.question: Is this a complete monitoring product?
faq.answer: No. It is a documentation skill with examples for building Python queries against Azure Monitor.
